page: Vultr vcg-mi355x-252c-2872g-2304vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server configured with an x86_64 AMD CPU architecture. The system features 252 physical cores and 252 vCPUs, operating at one thread per core with shared CPU allocation. System memory totals 2872.0 GB, providing approximately 11.4 GB of RAM per core.

The server is equipped with eight AMD CDNA4 MI355X GPUs, delivering a combined total of 2304 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of 14336 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. The network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

This hardware profile represents a high-density resource class designed for massive parallel processing. The consolidation of large-scale GPU memory, high-capacity system RAM, and local NVMe storage provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring extensive memory footprints and accelerated computing without the overhead of multi-node clustering. Appropriate workloads include large language model training, complex data science jobs, large-scale AI inference, and intensive database operations.
description: Vultr vcg-mi355x-252c-2872g-2304vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server featuring an x86_64 AMD processor with 252 physical cores and 252 vCPUs. The platform is equipped with 2872.0 GB of system memory, yielding 11.4 GB of RAM per core. Acceleration is driven by eight AMD CDNA4 MI355X GPUs, providing a total of 2304 GB of VRAM. For local storage, the system includes 14336 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. This high-density hardware configuration is designed for massive parallel processing tasks, offering qualitative cost efficiency by consolidating substantial compute, memory, and acceleration resources into a single bare-metal node. It is engineered for workloads such as large-scale AI model training, deep learning inference, data science pipelines, and memory-intensive database operations.
